behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths, identifiers (BUILT_IN_COMMANDS, useActiveConversation, isSkillsLoading, conversation_version === 'V1', /new) referenced by tests are named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to do (add BUILT_IN_COMMANDS, check V1, handle loading) but doesn't provide exact code; agent must still write it.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert specific literal strings like 'conversation_version === "V1"' and 'BUILT_IN_COMMANDS' and 'isSkillsLoading' in source text; a valid alternative (e.g., destructuring differently, using a constant with a different name, or checking version via a helper) would fail these text-based checks.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute npm test via subprocess and run actual vitest behavioral tests on the hook, in addition to some text assertions.

test_not_tautological.reason: The vitest tests render the hook and assert filteredItems contain/exclude /new based on V1/V0, which requires real implementation.

behavior_in_tests.reason: V1 inclusion, V0 exclusion, and loading state behaviors described in instruction are all covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly named and grouped with docstrings explaining fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file to modify, which is appropriate since the task is adding a feature rather than localizing a bug.
